mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, sustained restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as leveraged corporate cash flows are repriced lower.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) generate a reinforcing demand signal that exceeds the sum of individual effects, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces lower mean returns than the best single-proxy signal over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal contracting manufacturing output and imminent earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not occur in more than 55 percent of months with INDPRO declines during late-cycle periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal durable demand strength and construction sector profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through backward linkages to materials and employment.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, labor market deterioration triggers rapid risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not occur in at least 60 percent of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) creates a stagflationary impulse where input costs rise without output growth, compressing profit margins and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and falling INDPRO fails to underperform the unconditional market return by at least 50 basis points over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 3 percent annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports upward corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following high GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline with t-test p-value greater than 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.
